name: Honey-Mustard Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless, skinless chicken breasts (trimmed of all fat)
Tub-style margarine, canola or corn oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into small bite-sized pieces.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper if desired.
3. In a large nonstick skillet coated with oil, cook the chicken pieces over medium heat, turning frequently with a wooden or plastic spoon until they begin to brown and are cooked through. Remove and set aside in a bowl.
4. In the same skillet, combine margarine (or oil), onion, garlic, and 3 tablespoons broth. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently for about 6-7 minutes or until the onions are tender. If liquid begins to evaporate, add more broth.
5. Add honey and lemon juice to the pan with onions, stir to combine well.
6. Stir in the remaining chicken broth and salt (if desired).
7. Stir in the reserved chicken, cauliflower, and carrot. Bring to a boil.
8. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes or until flavors are blended.
9. Meanwhile, cook rice according to package directions.
10. In a small bowl, combine water and cornstarch. Stir into the simmering sauce and cook, stirring until thickened. Stir in mustard. Simmer an additional 1-2 minutes.
11. Arrange rice on a large serving platter. Top with chicken and sauce. Alternatively, serve individual portions over rice.
Judi's Notes: Cooking cauliflower for 20 minutes is too long; consider 10 minutes to maintain its bright color and prevent it from becoming mushy.
